message:
fix: use strings.LastIndex in ClearAlert to prevent panic on variable-length device types (#3289) (#3309)

ClearAlert() used hard-coded string slicing (len("/gpu")+1) to extract
the node key from alert IDs, which assumed all device type suffixes were
3 characters long. This caused incorrect key extraction for longer types
like "infiniband", "mofed-driver", "gpu-driver", and "spectrum-scale",
and could cause an out-of-bounds panic for short alert IDs.

Replace the brittle arithmetic with strings.LastIndex(alertID, "/") to
correctly split on the last slash regardless of suffix length. Also
extend the switch statement to handle all device types that
processSnapshot can generate alerts for.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
